methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $14.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in AI & Software. Range: $1.5B - $39.0B.

private.rationale: Same sector (AI & Software), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

data.narrative:
Skild AI is a Series C artificial intelligence and software company founded by Deepak Pathak and Abhinav Gupta, currently valued at $14.0B post-money following $1.4B+ in total capital raised. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies—one public (Palantir) and eight private—all operating in AI & Software at equivalent or adjacent stages.

Two triangulation methods apply to this valuation. Stage-progression modeling applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier typical of Series C advancement, yielding $23.8B. Peer-median valuation references four same-sector, same-stage comparables (Figure AI at $39.0B, Luma AI at $4.0B, Runway and Magic AI each at $1.5B), producing a median of $2.8B, though the range spans $1.5B to $39.0B—indicating wide dispersion in the cohort. These methods triangulate to a range of $2.3B (low) to $27.4B (high), with a median of $13.3B.

The current $14.0B valuation sits near the triangulated median, suggesting reasonable positioning within the derived range. Key uncertainties include the methodological gap between stage-progression and peer-median approaches—reflecting genuine heterogeneity in frontier AI company valuations—and the significant outlier effect of Figure AI's $39.0B valuation on the peer set. The analysis supports valuations between $2.3B and $27.4B, with the $14.0B mark representing the midpoint of this wide but defensible band.
